page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors. This instance features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz with a single thread per core, backed by 128.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and cached small-block memory read bandwidth both ranking in the top 25 percent. Software compilation via Clang, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also demonstrate strong performance. However, single-core execution is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core scores, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression falling into the bottom 25 percent. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression remain in the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides high resource density and strong multi-core throughput relative to its hardware profile, making it a viable alternative to x86-based compute instances. The high network bandwidth of 100 Gbps combined with strong multi-core performance makes this instance well-suited for network-intensive workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput static web serving, and distributed caching systems like Redis. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 architecture. Running at 2.5 GHz with one thread per core, it is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The server operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core CPU performance, high cached memory read bandwidth, and exceptional static web serving and Redis SET throughput. Conversely, single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks represent performance tradeoffs, scoring in the lower percentiles. This instance offers a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded, network-heavy applications, making it highly suitable for software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and distributed caching workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB of RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for multi-threaded and network-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and high-speed networking for multi-threaded and network-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers strong multi-core CPU, Redis, and static web serving benchmark performance
Tradeoffs include lower single-core performance and single-threaded compression speeds
Optimized for multi-threaded software compilation, web serving, and caching workloads
